page: UpCloud HIMEM-24xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7543 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 21.33 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 700 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core and single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng scores placing the instance in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU physics tests also demonstrate strong performance. Memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks is highly efficient, ranking in the top 25% at 93.44 GB/sec, while read-only memory bandwidth and database operations perform within the average range.

Although the CPU allocation is shared, the high memory-to-core ratio makes this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration. The absence of AVX-512 vector extensions limits its suitability for certain advanced scientific computing tasks. However, the combination of 512 GB of RAM and 700 GB of local SSD storage makes it well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise resource planning applications, and heavy data-processing pipelines.
description: UpCloud HIMEM-24xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC 7543 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 24 physical cores at 2.0 GHz alongside 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a substantial 21.33 GB of memory per core. The hardware configuration includes a 700 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core CPU performance and high-speed large-block memory write bandwidth, both ranking in the top 25%. While the shared CPU allocation and lack of AVX-512 support are notable tradeoffs, the server offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and complex data-processing workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual server featuring twenty-four AMD EPYC cores and a bundled seven-hundred gigabyte local solid-state drive.

512.0 GB of system memory providing 21.33 GB of RAM per core
